User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user in the tipping calculator app.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user (matches Firebase Auth UID).

email.description: The email address of the user.

shopName.description: The name of the user's shop.

upiId.description: The user's UPI ID for payments.

subscriptionStatus.description: The status of the user's subscription.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the calculation was made.

userId.description: The ID of the user who made the calculation.

KhataEntry.title: Khata Entry
KhataEntry.description: Represents a single entry in the Khata book.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Khata entry.

name.description: Name of the customer or supplier.

phone.description: Phone number of the customer or supplier.

amount.description: The amount due. Positive for receivables, negative for payables.

userId.description: The ID of the user this entry belongs to.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a single transaction within a Khata entry.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the transaction was made.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Path-based ownership for user data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores calculations for a specific user. Path-based ownership ensures that users can only access their own calculations.

params.name: calculationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the calculation.

definition.description: Stores khata book entries for a specific user.

params.name: khataEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the khata entry.

definition.description: Stores all transactions for a specific Khata entry.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the transaction.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to support a calculator application with user-specific history.  The top-level `users` collection stores basic user profiles.  A `calculations` subcollection is nested under each user's document. This approach enforces strong data ownership, as security rules can easily restrict access to a user's own `calculations` subcollection based on their UID in the path. This avoids the need for querying and filtering on a `userId` field in a global `calculations` collection, making security rules simpler and more performant.
